title: Culture should be specified for string operations
description: string.ToLower(), ToUpper, IndexOf, LastIndexOf, and Compare are all culture-dependent, as are some (floating point number and DateTime-related) calls to ToString. Fortunately, all have variants which accept an argument specifying the culture or formatter to use. Leave that argument off and the call will use the system default culture, possibly creating problems with international characters.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

noncompliant:
// Noncompliant: Using default culture
string result = input.ToLower();
compliant:
// Compliant: Specifying invariant culture
string result = input.ToLower(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);

// Compliant: Using ToUpperInvariant
string result = input.ToUpperInvariant();

logic.detection_conditions: Assignments or conditions containing culture-sensitive string operations (ToLower, ToUpper, IndexOf, LastIndexOf, Compare) without culture specification
logic.triggering_conditions: (ToLower() OR ToUpper() OR IndexOf(...) OR LastIndexOf(...) OR string.Compare(...)) AND NOT (CultureInfo OR StringComparison OR Invariant) AND NOT in logging context
logic.exclusion_conditions: Exclude operations that already specify CultureInfo or StringComparison, ToUpperInvariant/ToLowerInvariant methods, logging contexts (Console.*, Logger.*), string formatting
never_trigger_on: Methods already specifying CultureInfo or StringComparison parameters
logic.reasoning: String operations like ToLower, ToUpper, IndexOf, LastIndexOf, and Compare are culture-dependent and can produce unexpected results with international characters. The rule targets Assignment nodes which capture variable assignments and condition expressions. Methods with 'Invariant' suffix are excluded as they're already culture-safe. Logging and display contexts are excluded. ToString detection is omitted due to difficulty in type tracking without semantic analysis.
